explanation: The sentence describes that when the ventilator is in the Inspiration state (which is mapped to 'inspiratoryPhase'), then two things must hold: (1) the out valve is closed (mapped to 'outValveClose'), and (2) the in valve pressure must be set to the appropriate target (which, separately, is mapped to either P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV, P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV, or P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP depending on the mode, but here, generically, we'll refer to a disjunction of all three). The requirement is global: it must ALWAYS be the case when inspiratoryPhase holds. Thus, we write: G(inspiratoryPhase -> (outValveClose & (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP))).

explanation: CONT39_3 specifies: If in inspiratory phase AND in apnea backup in PCV mode, then in valve pressure is set to P_inspAP. The state 'PCV from apnea backup' can be reasonably mapped to PCVMode & apnea. So, the formula is: G((inspiratoryPhase & PCVMode & apnea) -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP).

explanation:
The requirement states: Whenever the ventilator is in the Inspiration state (which we assume is represented by 'inspiratoryPhase'), then two things must hold during that phase: (1) the out valve shall be closed (assume 'outValveClose' holds), and (2) the in valve pressure shall be set to the target inspiratory pressure according to the mode. The three subcases specify what the 'target inspiratory pressure' means depending on the mode: PCVMode => P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV, PSVMode => P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV, PCVMode from apnea backup => P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP. For the general spec, let's capture the guard (being in 'inspiratoryPhase') and, while there, insist that ('outValveClose' holds) and (one of those three pressure setpoints holds according to the mode).

Thus, globally, if inspiratoryPhase holds, then (outValveClose is true) and (depending on the mode, the in-valve pressure is set appropriately). Since the modes are atomic propositions provided, we can use implications per mode. The in-valve pressure requirement becomes: (PCVMode -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV) & (PSVMode -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV) & ((apnea detected and PCVMode) -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP). Note: For PCV after apnea backup, we must check for that condition (e.g., PCVMode & apnea), but since only 'apnea' is not an AP, we may use the given AP 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP' as indicating the right set point is chosen. For the LTL, we'll prioritize the APs provided.

So, the specification:
G ( inspiratoryPhase -> ( outValveClose & ( (PCVMode -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV) & (PSVMode -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV) & (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP) ) ) )

Where the last conjunct (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP) is necessary if, under apnea backup, the ventilator automatically sets the setpoint. This covers that at any inspiration, if PCVMode then correct pressure, if PSVMode then correct pressure, and in any case, if inspiration is from backup, then proper set point also holds.

explanation: The requirement describes: when the ventilator is in the Inspiration state, (1) the out valve must be closed (outValveClose), and (2) the in valve pressure must be set to a target inspiratory pressure, which depends on the current ventilation mode (P_insp equal to P_inspPCV, P_inspPSV, or P_inspAP according to the active mode: PCV, PSV, or PCV from apnea backup). So, the implication 'if inspiratoryPhase then outValveClose and the correct inspiratory pressure is selected according to the mode' must always hold globally. That is, globally, whenever 'inspiratoryPhase' holds, the conjunction of 'outValveClose' and the right 'P_insp = P_inspXYZ' according to the current mode must hold. This can be stated as: G(inspiratoryPhase -> (outValveClose & (PCVMode -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV) & (PSVMode -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV) & (PCVMode & apnea -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP))). The PCV-from-apnea-backup case is encoded as PCVMode & apnea. The formula is a conjunction of all mode-dependent cases under the guard of inspiratoryPhase.

explanation:
The natural language sentence expresses a requirement applying whenever the ventilator is in the 'Inspiration state', which corresponds to 'inspiratoryPhase' being true. In that case, the 'out valve shall be closed' (modeled as 'outValveClose') and 'the in valve pressure shall be set to the target inspiratory pressure': this target varies depending on the mode, and maps to exactly one of 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV', or 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP'. These three cases can be summarized as a disjunction (since in each mode, only one applies). So, when 'inspiratoryPhase' holds, both 'outValveClose' is true and exactly one of the pressure-setting signals is true. We express this using implication and globally (G) since the requirement should hold whenever inspiration is active during system execution. 

Thus, the LTL formula is: G(inspiratoryPhase -> (outValveClose & (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP)))

explanation: The requirement specifies: Whenever the ventilator is in the 'Inspiration' state (represented by atomic proposition 'inspiratoryPhase'), then two things must hold: (1) the out valve is closed ('outValveClose') and (2) the in valve pressure shall be set to the target inspiratory pressure. The target inspiratory pressure is split into three subrequirements: in PCV mode, it is 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV'; in PSV mode, 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV'; in PCV-from-apnea-backup, 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP'. Thus, whenever 'inspiratoryPhase' is true, 'outValveClose' must be true and also exactly one of the three inspiratory pressure settings must hold depending on the mode. Since these are mandates applying at all times (globally), we wrap them in a G (always) operator. The full formula is G(inspiratoryPhase -> (outValveClose & (PCVMode -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV) & (PSVMode -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV) & ((PCVMode & apnea) -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP))). The mapping relies on assumptions that PCV-from-apnea-backup can be expressed as 'PCVMode & apnea'.

explanation:
The requirement describes the behavior when the ventilator is in the Inspiration state (modeled by the atomic proposition 'inspiratoryPhase'). In this state, the out valve must stay closed (outValveClose) and the in valve pressure must be set to a target value, reflected by one of three atomic propositions depending on the mode (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV for PCV mode, P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV for PSV mode, P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP for backup PCV mode). Since these three cases are mutually exclusive based on the current mode (PCV, PSV, or PCV from apnea backup) but the main requirement is when the system is in inspiratory phase, we can express the global constraint as: globally, if we're in inspiratory phase, the out valve is closed and the in valve pressure is set to (at least) one of those three mode-specific values.

This can be formalized as:
G(inspiratoryPhase -> (outValveClose & (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP)))

explanation:
The CONT39 requirement states: When the ventilator is in an Inspiration state, then the out valve shall be closed (outValveClose holds) and the in valve pressure set to the target inspiratory pressure based on the mode. This means: whenever 'inspiratoryPhase' holds (which we use as 'Inspiration state'), then both 'outValveClose' holds, and the pressure is set to one of three target options: P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV (for PCV mode), P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV (for PSV mode), or P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP (for PCV from apnea backup). 

Therefore, the formalization is: Globally, if inspiratoryPhase holds, then outValveClose holds and (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV or P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV or P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP) holds. This is captured as G(inspiratoryPhase -> (outValveClose & (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP))).
